senses.gloss: The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include

examples.text: تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ الْفُرْقَانَ عَلَى عَبْدِهِ لِيَكُونَ لِلْعَالَمِينَ نَذِيرًا
examples.english: Blessed is He who sent down the Furqān upon His Servant for him to be to the worlds a warner.

etymology.text: Borrowed from Classical Syriac ܦܘܪܩܢܐ (purqānā) or Hebrew פֻּרְקָן (purqān).

body.text: , originally an inf. n. (Msb. [See 1, first sentence.])
Book of the Law revealed to Moses,
(Az, O, K,) in which a distinction is made between that which is allowable and that which is forbidden. (O.) ―         -b4-  And
The time a little before daybreak:
(IDrd, O, K:) so, accord. to IDrd, in the phrase
in the Kur [viii. 42]: (O:) or by this phrase is meant
(O, K,) in which a distinction was made between right and wrong. (O.) ―         -b7-  And
so it means [accord. to some] in the Kur ii. 50. (O, K.) ―         -b8-  And
(O, K:) such the people of the olden time used to make witnesses [in law-suits or the like]. (O.)       -A2-  It is also pl. of
